title: Fermented American Grapes Bake
description: A lacto-vegetarian-friendly, meal-prep dish featuring fermented grapes and a glassy-jelly texture, perfect for a weeknight dinner or breakfast. This recipe uses a pressure cooker for efficient cooking.
instructions: Preheat the pressure cooker to high pressure, and set the timer to 10 minutes.
Add the olive oil, then sauté the halved grapes until they start to soften and release their juices.
Add the honey, apple cider vinegar, salt, and black pepper to the pressure cooker. Stir to combine.
Add the water and oats to the pressure cooker. Stir to combine.
Close the pressure cooker lid, and ensure the valve is set to "sealing".
Cook for 10 minutes at high pressure.
Quick-release the pressure, then carefully open the lid.
Stir in the unsweetened almond milk.
Continue cooking the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the oats have absorbed most of the liquid and the mixture has a thick, jelly-like consistency.
Remove from heat, and let the Fermented American Grapes Bake cool before serving.
